capability_statement: AOF can now make release value comprehension itself a governed runtime outcome instead of relying on after-the-fact AI summaries.
before_state: Users had to infer value from records, audits, schemas, gates, projections, and long release histories.
after_state: Users can read capability-first release value evidence, a before/after comparison, a five-minute demo, and Mission Control understanding projection.
scenario: A user asks what changed from v9.0 to v9.1 and can answer from the capability matrix before reading implementation details.
five_minute_demo: Open docs/v9.1-product-value-comprehension.md, read the capability matrix, then run product-value-evidence-audit to verify the value evidence exists.
time_saved_or_work_reduced: Users no longer need a custom AI summary just to understand the release delta.
cognitive_load_removed: Users do not need to mentally translate record/audit/schema/gate/projection names into product capability.

capability_matrix.before: technical verification could pass without before/after scenario
capability_matrix.after: scenario, five-minute demo, work reduction, and cognitive-load reduction are required

capability_matrix.before: unclear value feedback stayed outside runtime
capability_matrix.after: unclear value can trigger self-audit, task, evidence, and release gate
capability_matrix.user_value: AOF can improve when users do not understand it

not_proven: This evidence proves bounded release value explanation and local understanding structure only; it does not prove market adoption, broad user satisfaction, semantic correctness, or production provider safety.
